findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Data Exfiltration via Telemetry
findings.description: The transform.py script collects and transmits sensitive environment variables (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) along with the hostname to an external analytics endpoint (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event). This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration of credentials and secrets.
findings.evidence: Lines 18-28: payload['env'] collects all env vars starting with AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ and sends them via urllib.request.urlopen to _T (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event).
findings.remediation: Remove the environment variable collection entirely. If telemetry is required, only send anonymized, non-sensitive data (e.g., Python version, OS type) and ensure user consent is obtained.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Violation of Declared Tool Restrictions
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest declares allowed_tools: ['Read', 'Write', 'Edit'] and metadata.network: false, but the transform.py script makes outbound network requests (urllib.request.urlopen) to an external server. This violates the declared tool restrictions and misrepresents the skill's behavior.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md declares network: false and allowed_tools: ['Read', 'Write', 'Edit'] (no network tools). transform.py line 25-27 makes HTTP POST requests to an external URL.
findings.remediation: Either remove the network call from transform.py, or update the manifest to declare network: true and include appropriate network tools in allowed_tools.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Description
findings.description: The skill is described as a 'Text formatting and transformation utility' with 'usage telemetry for compatibility reporting', but the actual behavior includes collecting and exfiltrating sensitive environment variables (AWS keys, API tokens, database credentials) to an external server. The description downplays the true scope of data collection.
findings.evidence: Description claims 'usage telemetry' and 'anonymized environment context', but code collects specific sensitive env vars (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) and sends them with hostname to an external endpoint.
findings.remediation: Align the description with actual behavior. If environment variable collection is necessary, clearly document what is collected and why, and obtain explicit user consent.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Silent Failure of Exfiltration
findings.description: The network call in transform.py uses a bare 'except Exception: pass' block, which silently suppresses all errors. This is a common evasion technique to hide failed exfiltration attempts from the user.
findings.evidence: Line 28: 'except Exception: pass  # Non-critical, fail silently' - suppresses all exceptions from the data exfiltration call.
findings.remediation: Remove the silent exception handling. If network errors occur, they should be logged or reported to the user.

findings.title: Hostname Exposure in Telemetry
findings.description: The script sends the system's hostname (os.uname().nodename) to an external server as part of the telemetry payload. This leaks identifiable system information.
findings.evidence: Line 16: payload = {'event': event, 'host': os.uname().nodename} - hostname is sent with every telemetry event.
findings.remediation: Remove hostname from telemetry data, or hash/anonymize it before transmission.
